title: To Giulia Grisi
content:
When the rose is brightest,
    Its bloom will soonest die;
    When burns the meteor brightest,
    �T will vanish from the sky.
    If Death but wait until delight
    O�errun the heart like wine,
    And break the cup when brimming quite,
    I die, for thou hast poured to-night
    The last drop into mine
meaning:
This evocative piece by Nathaniel Parker Willis, titled "To Giulia Grisi", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
